dataSourceNote: Slack: all #mon-prosp-* and #mon-partner-* channels + #prosp-monarch-deals. Granola: CLS Health (5/5 demo confirmed + pricing details), GLOC (full deal history + Jim Stilley profile), Premier Ortho (Assort contract context). No Granola data for Plymouth Bay, Total Primary Care, OS1, or Elevate. No Gong access.

executiveSummaries.size: 4 locs (2 pilot to start), ~3,500 calls/month (~7K appointments/month); free pilot → $1.25/resolved call (Maya) + $0.75/visit (ClearPay) at scale with ~15% design partner discount
executiveSummaries.champion: Jim Stilley (CEO, former Navy Commander), Deanna Dean (Clinical Manager, 10 years)
executiveSummaries.status: Signed 4/27 — Solv's first Monarch design partner. Channel renamed to mon-partner-gloc on 5/1. Kickoff doc live; implementation underway. Carli (ClearPay/Athena) collecting API credentials and requesting a preview instance copy-down. Kennedy (Maya) gathering phone routing setup and DID requirements. Jim's leadership team is on vacation and unavailable until ~May 16 — formal kickoff call targeted after that date. Kapeesh requested a Google Drive SOP to document all design partner onboarding learnings as a template for future deals.
keyRisks: Leadership unavailable until ~May 16 — implementation can't fully kick off for ~2 more weeks
Resolution rate unproven in ortho — free pilot pricing locked before we know the numbers
DID phone line may not exist at GLOC yet — provisioning takes 24-96 hours
executiveSummaries.nextStep: GLOC to provide Athena credentials + complete 3rd-party auth form; Kennedy to confirm DID phone status — Owner: Carli/Kennedy — Due: ASAP; formal kickoff call ~May 16

executiveSummaries.size: $3,000–$3,500/month platform fee (up to 3K telemed visits) + $0.50/ClearPay + 1% SolvPay; 15–30 telehealth physicians to start, 250–300+ providers total, Texas/Houston
executiveSummaries.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager, day-to-day), Dr. Khaled Attia (clinical champion); Dr. Baba = skeptical decision-maker; President of CLS engaged and driving urgency
executiveSummaries.status: Full telehealth end-to-end demo delivered 5/5 (Granola + Slack confirmed). Dr. Baba explicitly gating on ECW integration proof — 'if this doesn't integrate with ECW, let us know.' HMO/PCP routing unresolved (proposed workaround: place of service 20 or cash-pay). Luma disqualified by CLS for not matching dynamic eligibility. President wants Solv to potentially replace Luma for ALL primary care scheduling — major scope expansion beyond the original telehealth motion. CLS is committing a full-time internal project resource. As of 5/6, Katie asked Danny and Brantley to compile all CLS feedback/questions into a doc for Maria Kwok (now on channel) to review. Target go-live June 1; 6-8 week implementation puts that at risk.
keyRisks: ECW integration proof is the hard gate — Dr. Baba needs a demo video or reference (ECW + multi-provider + Texas) within 1-2 days
HMO/assigned PCP billing workaround unresolved — could block a meaningful patient segment at launch
Pricing negotiation open — CLS wants hybrid model, 6-month first term, and waived $2K impl fee simultaneously
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Deliver ECW integration proof (video or ECW + multi-provider + Texas reference) + compile CLS feedback/questions doc for Maria Kwok — Owner: Brantley/Danny — Due: Today/tomorrow

executiveSummaries.size: 3 existing locs + 1 standalone ortho urgent care opening June 1; MRR TBD
executiveSummaries.champion: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne/ex-Solv user), Christopher LaFave (Executive Director)
executiveSummaries.status: Strong 4/8 executive demo — Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, and Dir. of Marketing all attended. Team confirmed urgent ortho workflow fit and gave verbal interest. June 1 new location is the forcing function. Christopher pushed for a live ECW data-flow demo before committing. Deck and ECW integration packet were due Mon/Tues of the week of 4/14 — now approximately 3 weeks overdue with no update in channel. Referral workflow gap remains a risk for expansion beyond the urgent care location.
keyRisks: ECW live-data-flow demo is a hard gate — Christopher won't commit without seeing it
Deck + ECW integration packet is ~3 weeks overdue — momentum loss risk is real
Referral workflow gap blocks expansion to the 3 non-urgent ortho clinics (HMO patients require referral before scheduling)
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Confirm follow-up call is booked and send ECW integration packet immediately — Owner: Brantley — Due: Overdue, chase today

executiveSummaries.size: 47 confirmed locations, ECW, homegrown scheduler; acquisition-layer scenario only
executiveSummaries.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff) — not final DM
executiveSummaries.status: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. Rikin won't replace the CTO-built scheduler ('too many resources poured in') and is 6-9 months from any real timeline (earliest October 2026). Only viable path is Solv as an acquisition layer alongside their existing scheduler — a non-standard deal structure with unknown internal appetite. Rikin is also actively piloting a competing voice agent and dismissed Maya. Teresa and Kapeesh are aware. Team can close this out.
keyRisks: 6-9 month stated timeline puts any deal in Q4 2026 at earliest
Competing voice agent in active pilot — Maya is closed for now
Non-standard acquisition-only model requires internal Solv go/no-go before re-engaging
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Internal Solv decision: pursue acquisition-only model at scale or formally close out — Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh — Due: TBD

executiveSummaries.champion: Jon Borja (COO, proactive communicator) — knows Maya from prior Exer implementation
executiveSummaries.status: Paused since 3/11 due to unresolved Assort Health contract exit. Jon proactively communicated the block rather than ghosting and confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. This deal was near-signature in late February (DocuSign pending) before Assort surfaced. Assort's ToS prevents running Maya in parallel — full exit required before activation. Last active outreach was 3/30 (friendly check-in, no asks). Bryan joined the channel 4/22. Per the every-2-weeks cadence, a check-in is overdue as of early May.
keyRisks: Zero visibility into Assort exit timeline — could be weeks or months
Deal was near-signature in Feb; restarting after a 2-month pause requires re-qualification
Competitive risk grows silently during the pause window
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Overdue check-in with Jon on Assort exit status — Owner: Brantley — Due: This week

executiveSummaries.champion: Randy Johansen (Board Member, former AFC CEO) — intro via Heather at UCA happy hour
executiveSummaries.status: Note: Based on #prosp-monarch-deals only — no dedicated channel or Granola data found. First exploratory call 4/24. Org opened 3 locations simultaneously and cash-constrained itself; Kennesaw (Atlanta) location is a serious drag (opened without MRI for months). Randy was meeting with the OS1 team to assess readiness the week of 4/28 — no update posted in channel since 4/24. Planned reconnect (Friday 5/1 or Monday 5/4) has now passed with no visible follow-through. Status unknown.
keyRisks: Cash constraints may make any new spend a non-starter before operations stabilize
Randy is a board member, not an operator — internal decision path unclear
Reconnect week of 4/28 has passed with no visible update — deal may have gone cold
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Confirm status of Randy/OS1 reconnect call and share ClearPay + Maya materials — Owner: Brantley/Evan — Due: Overdue, confirm today

executiveSummaries.champion: Katia Sarfian (Administrator) — Dr. Tovmassian (owner) is the blocker
executiveSummaries.status: Note: ClearPay-only deal — not a Monarch bundle. Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner, also owns existing Solv partner Mederi) rejected ClearPay on 3/16: 'doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% of patients are recurring.' Real collection pain exists but the point-of-care estimate workflow doesn't fit primary care recurring patients under current pricing. No response to 3/24 follow-up. Not pushing aggressively to protect the existing Mederi relationship.
keyRisks: Owner-level rejection is substantive — recurring primary care model doesn't fit current ClearPay pricing
Pushing too hard risks damaging the active Mederi partnership
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Dormant — revisit only if ClearPay evolves a primary care pricing model — Owner: Brantley — Due: No near-term action

leadershipTable.keyNotes: Signed 4/27; implementation live — Athena API access + Maya DID setup underway; kickoff ~May 16.
5/5 demo done; ECW integration proof is hard gate — Dr. Baba needs video/reference today/tomorrow.
Strong 4/8 exec demo; ECW live-data-flow demo overdue ~3 weeks — June 1 is the forcing function.
6-9 month timeline; won't replace homegrown scheduler; competing voice agent in pilot — close or defer.
Blocked by Assort contract since 3/11; check-in overdue — Jon will re-engage when exit resolves.
Post-UCA board intro; reconnect week of 4/28 has passed with no visible update — confirm status.
Owner rejected ClearPay outright — primary care recurring-patient model isn't a product fit.

closedLost.reason: Ghosted — no response after multiple follow-ups since March 9. Admin contact (Osvaldo) was bought in but providers were the real decision-makers and never engaged. Closed out by Brantley 4/7.
Deal broken off by Solv — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility made ClearPay integration infeasible. Pricing model also mismatched (2 rotating providers across 6 locations vs. per-location pricing).
